meta.tagline: Santa Clarita's #1-ranked moving + storage company on Google Maps
meta.author: Aleksandar Spasevski
meta.doc_subtitle: Defend #1 Santa Clarita Maps position · capitalize on packing/storage momentum · expand into outer service-area cities.

tldr: AQMS is the #1-ranked Santa Clarita mover on Google Maps by every measure we track — averaging position 3.6 across 350 grid cells in the May 18 scan, with 4.9 stars and 452 reviews (up from 441 at engagement start). Since March 8 the position has been held, not gained — net −15 top-3 placements across the three tracked queries, concentrated in local movers. The bright spot is packing & storage, which moved up +5 top-3 placements and gained nearly a full position (avg rank 4.73 → 3.82). Three competitors are pushing hard: Camelot +47 top-3, Precision +31, and Victory +20. The Q3 plan is three levers — defend the local-movers position, capitalize on packing/storage momentum, and break into the outer service-area cities that the current single-pin reach doesn't touch.

kpis.label: AQMS overall avg rank
kpis.detail: Across 350 cells / 3 tracked queries (May 18). #1 in market — next-best competitor (J&G) at 6.32; biggest threat (Camelot) at 6.62.

kpis.label: Top-3 placements (Mar 8 → May 18)
kpis.detail: Net −15 across the 3 time-series queries. Holding the dominant share, but Camelot (+47), Precision (+31), and Victory (+20) are closing on share they didn't have in March.

kpis.label: Reviews · rating
kpis.detail: +11 reviews since Mar 8 (441 → 452) at sustained 4.9-star average. Healthy review velocity ~4.4/mo — ahead of all tracked competitors.

kpis.label: Packing & storage momentum
kpis.detail: Best-moving query in the set. Avg rank 4.73 → 3.82 (gained 0.91 positions). May 3 scan peaked at 70 top-3 cells.

kpis.label: Local movers regression
kpis.detail: 78 → 65 top-3 cells. Avg rank 3.26 → 3.95 (−0.69). Still #1 in market but the position is softening; the trend across all 4 scans is choppy (78 → 78 → 88 → 65).

kpis.label: Managed locations
kpis.detail: Santa Clarita HQ (25701 Springbrook Ave). Service area covers 20 cities but the single pin's effective Maps reach decays sharply past ~5 mi. Outer cities (Moorpark, Simi Valley, Castaic) need a different play.

maps_presence.action: Defend — review velocity, post cadence, photo refresh, attributes

ld_leaderboard.name: Camelot Moving & Storage, Inc.
ld_leaderboard.note: Biggest gainer (+47) — active SEO/GBP work likely. Watch closely.

ld_leaderboard.name: J&G Full Service Moving
ld_leaderboard.note: Biggest loser (−24) — dropped in May 18 scan; ranks now sliding

ld_leaderboard.name: Precision Moving & Storage Corp.
ld_leaderboard.note: New entrant pushing hard (+31) — +46 cells of coverage

ld_leaderboard.name: RC Moving Company
ld_leaderboard.note: Wide coverage, low top-3 conversion; flat

movers_baseline_note.note: Single-baseline scan. Not in 4-date time series; included as a reference point for breadth coverage at engagement start.

positives: #1 in market by every Maps metric — avg rank 3.6, 224 top-3 placements across 350 cells / 3 queries
4.9-star rating with 452 reviews (+11 since engagement start) — healthiest review velocity in the local set
Packing & storage avg rank improved 4.73 → 3.82 (−0.91 positions, +5 top-3 cells)
Moving company near me held steady at avg rank 3.05 — the highest-intent commercial query still owned
5 GBP posts published 2026 YTD (Jan, Feb, Mar, Apr, May 20) — consistent monthly cadence with locally-relevant content (Santa Clarita, hidden costs, packing services)
30-year track record (founded 1996) + Mayflower affiliation + commercial / international / office capabilities give real depth competitors don't match
Active social presence (YouTube + Facebook + Instagram) tied back into GBP — trust signals reinforced
negatives: Top-3 share down 15 cells net (239 → 224) since engagement start — mostly on local movers (−13) and moving company near me (−7)
Local movers avg rank slipped 3.26 → 3.95 (−0.69) — still #1 in market, but pattern across 4 scans is choppy (3.26 → 3.60 → 3.09 → 3.95)
Camelot Moving & Storage gained +47 top-3 cells in 2.5 months — active SEO/GBP work; their avg rank improved 8.30 → 6.62
Two new entrants (Precision Moving +31, Victory Moving +20) are building share in the grid — territory that was uncontested in March
Single managed GBP at Santa Clarita HQ — 5 outer-service-area cities (Castaic, Simi Valley, San Fernando, Moorpark, Agua Dulce) are outside effective Maps reach radius
Service area pages on aqmsnationalmoving.com not yet built for each declared service-area city — missed organic capture in the cities the Santa Clarita pin can't reach

levers.name: Defend the #1 Santa Clarita Maps position
levers.why: AQMS is #1 in market but has lost 15 top-3 cells in 2.5 months while Camelot has gained 47. The defense playbook is straightforward GBP hygiene + content velocity + review-acquisition discipline, layered on the strongest brand in the local set.

actions.name: Audit + optimize Santa Clarita HQ GBP profile (description refresh, attributes, products, services match-back, photo refresh — fresh photos every 30d)
Lock in 2x/month GBP post cadence (one informational, one offer/seasonal) — current cadence is 1/month
Review-acquisition campaign: post-job SMS request + Google review link on invoice/email signature. Target +20 reviews by end of M3.
Q&A seeding on the GBP profile (10 common Santa Clarita-moving questions seeded with answers)
Build/strengthen /local-movers/ + /moving-company-near-me/ pages on aqmsnationalmoving.com — these are the queries losing top-3 share
Weekly Maps grid monitoring (DataForSEO LD pull) — set alert if avg rank moves > 0.5 positions in either direction

levers.outcome: Top-3 placement count recovers to 240+ by end of M3. Local movers avg rank pulls back to 3.3-3.5. Camelot's +47 gain stalls.

levers.name: Capitalize on packing & storage momentum
levers.why: Packing & storage is the one query AQMS is gaining on (+5 top-3, avg rank 4.73 → 3.82). The Storage Facility + Self Storage Facility GBP categories already exist. Doubling down here creates a second revenue stream alongside moving — and storage is recurring-revenue, unlike the one-time moving job.

actions.name: Build dedicated /storage-units-santa-clarita/ page with full pricing + unit-size matrix + climate/security features + virtual tour
Build dedicated /packing-services-santa-clarita/ page with packing-tier matrix (full / partial / fragile-only), supplies list, sample inventory checklist
Blog: 'How much storage do you need? Santa Clarita unit-size calculator' (utility content, internal-links to /storage-units-santa-clarita/)
Blog: 'Full vs partial packing service in Santa Clarita: what's worth paying for?' (commercial-intent, links to /packing-services-santa-clarita/)
Schema markup deploy on both pages (LocalBusiness + Service + Product/Offer where applicable) + FAQ schema
Add Storage + Packing GBP posts monthly (separate cadence from moving posts)

levers.outcome: Packing & storage avg rank moves to 3.2 or better. Storage page ranks top-10 on 'storage units santa clarita' by M3. Recurring storage revenue contribution measurable in client reporting.

levers.name: Break into outer service-area cities
levers.why: AQMS declares 20 service-area cities but the single Santa Clarita pin's effective Maps reach is ~5 mi. Castaic, Simi Valley, San Fernando, Moorpark, Agua Dulce, and the Antelope Valley edge all show as zero-reach in the LD grid (pin out of radius). The fix is dedicated service-area pages + Driving Direction pins to seed those city searches.

actions.name: Build /movers-castaic-ca/ service-area page (real content, Castaic-specific testimonials, local landmarks/HOA references, schema)
Seed 4 Driving Directions pins on the GBP (Castaic / Simi Valley / San Fernando / Moorpark) — directs traffic and proximity signal to outer cells
Local citation deploy for the new SA pages on Yelp / BBB / YellowPages / Foursquare / local chamber sites (one per city)

levers.outcome: AQMS visible on Maps for 'movers castaic' / 'movers simi valley' etc. by M3 (target top-10 in each). Organic traffic to /movers-{city}-ca/ pages contributes incremental lead volume not currently captured.

owner_asks: Authorization to deploy a review-acquisition workflow (post-job SMS / email signature link) — needs sign-off on customer communication
Confirmation of full 20-city service-area priority (which 4 are most strategically important for outer-pin builds in M1-M2)
Access confirmation: GBP managed by HQDM or shared management? (need write access for post cadence + Q&A seeding + DD pin seeding)
Are there any locations or services that are seasonal / off-roadmap so we don't waste content effort?
